zh.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graff是Caspari Graff和Annae Mariae Negel的女兒，於1735年出生在巴登。她於1758年與Johann Francis Lucas Jacoby在巴登阿赤 福包格沙爾斯塔特門根結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graff是Caspari Graff和Annae Mariae Negel的女儿，于1735年出生在巴登。她于1758年与Johann Francis Lucas Jacoby在巴登阿赤 福包格沙爾斯塔特門根结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Graff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

variants.name: Graf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Groff
meanings: Americanized form of Hungarian and Slovak Gróf, Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Grof ‘count’. The surname Groff is also found in Hungary, where it is very rare.
Americanized form of Norwegian Grov: habitational name from any of several farmsteads so called, from Old Norse grǫf ‘hollow, depression’.

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

nameMeanings.name: Maria
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graff was born in 1735, in Baden as the daughter of Caspari Graff and Annae Mariae Negel. She married Johann Francis Lucas Jacoby in 1758, in Mengen, Schallstadt, Amt Freiburg, Baden. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graff est née en 1735, à Baden, fille de Caspari Graff et Annae Mariae Negel. Elle a épousé Johann Francis Lucas Jacoby en 1758, à Mengen, Schallstadt, Amt Freiburg, Baden. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graff wurde 1735 in Baden, als Tochter von Caspari Graff und Annae Mariae Negel geboren. Sie hat 1758 in Mengen, Schallstadt, Amt Freiburg, Baden Johann Francis Lucas Jacoby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graff è nata il nel 1735 nel Baden come figlia di Caspari Graff e Annae Mariae Negel. Lei sposato Johann Francis Lucas Jacoby nel 1758, nel Mengen, Schallstadt, Amt Freiburg, Baden. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graffは1735年にバデンでCaspari GraffとAnnae Mariae Negelの娘として生まれた。1758年にバデンアムト フレイブルグスチャルスタトメンジェンでJohann Francis Lucas Jacobyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graff(은)는 1735년 바든에서 Caspari Graff(와)과 Annae Mariae Negel의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1758년 바든 아트 프레이버그 샤스타드트 멘겐에서 Johann Francis Lucas Jacoby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graff nasceu em 1735, em Baden filha de Caspari Graff e Annae Mariae Negel. Ela casou-se com Johann Francis Lucas Jacoby em 1758, em Mengen, Schallstadt, Amt Freiburg, Baden. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graff родилась в 1735 г. в местности баден. Её родителей звали Caspari Graff и Annae Mariae Negel. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Johann Francis Lucas Jacoby в 1758 г. в местности менген, щоллстадт, амт фрибург, баден. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Anna Maria Graff nació en 1735, en Baden como hija de Caspari Graff y Annae Mariae Negel. Se casó con Johann Francis Lucas Jacoby en 1758, en Mengen, Schallstadt, Amt Freiburg, Baden. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas.
